old_claim.note: Chemical Assay Laboratories provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No fire-assay workshop dependency remains because metallurgical recipes are one subject in a broad popular print genre, not its production infrastructure.

source_shape.source_locator: Description lists medicine, food preservation, cosmetics, inks, stains, and chemical refining as recipe subjects and classifies the material form as print.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Books of secrets compile many household and craft instructions for readers in a printed popular genre.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Content about chemical work does not imply that a specialized 1556 assay workshop caused a 1497 printed recipe book.

ontology_before: Printed recipe books depended on a later specialized metallurgical workshop because some recipes discussed metallurgy.
ontology_after: The printed genre depends on printing; its varied recipe subjects remain content rather than prerequisites.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: chemical_recipe_books no longer directly depends on chemical_assay_laboratories.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to a manual demonstrably produced inside and for a specific fire-assay workshop.
short_reason: A recipe subject is not publishing infrastructure.
